question: What is the difference between worst-case and average-case analysis of an algorithm?
answer: The difference between worst-case and average-case analysis of an algorithm lies in their objectives. Worst-case analysis examines the longest possible running time that an algorithm can take on any input, providing an upper bound on the running time. Average-case analysis, however, considers the expected running time over all possible inputs, assuming they are equally likely, giving a more realistic expectation of the algorithm's performance on practical inputs.

Average-case analysis, on the other hand, looks at the expected running time over all possible inputs, assuming they are equally likely. While worst-case analysis guarantees the algorithm will never take longer than this bound, average-case analysis gives a more realistic expectation of the algorithm's performance on practical inputs.
